def_en: A narrow strip of land connecting two larger landmasses.
quote_en: The Isthmus of Panama connects North and South America; it also has a famous canal.
quote_zh: A narrow isthmus of sand linked the mainland to the island, but high tide could cover it.
ex_en: An isthmus is a bridge; it reminds us that no land is truly separate.
ex_zh: We are all isthmuses connecting past to future, one shore to another.

quote_en: To keep the balloon from crashing, the pilot had to jettison the sandbags.
quote_zh: We had to jettison our plan to camp when the rain started, but the indoor fort was just as fun.
ex_en: Sometimes you must jettison what weighs you down to rise higher.
ex_zh: Let go of what holds you back; the boat sails lighter without useless cargo.

quote_en: The camp counselor was in a jocular mood, telling jokes that made everyone laugh around the fire.
quote_zh: A jocular remark from the teacher eased the tension before the big test.
ex_en: A jocular spirit is a gift; laughter turns heavy moments into light.
ex_zh: Keep a jocular heart; it’s the oil that makes the gears of friendship run smoothly.

def_en: A fastening made by tying; a tangle.
def_zh: tie a knot; knot in a rope
quote_en: The sailor taught me how to tie a knot that would hold even in a strong wind.
quote_zh: There was a knot in my shoelace that took ten minutes to undo.
ex_en: A knot can be a strong bond or a tangled mess—it all depends on how you tie it.
ex_zh: Some knots in life must be patiently untangled; others, you learn to work with.

def_en: A rope with a noose; a lasso.
quote_en: The cowboy swung his lariat over his head and caught the runaway calf on the first try.
quote_zh: At the rodeo, we watched a demonstration of how to use a lariat to rope a steer.
ex_en: A lariat is a tool of skill, not force; it catches not by strength but by precision.
ex_zh: Sometimes you need to throw a lariat of kindness to pull someone back from the edge.

quote_en: The doctor’s office had an information leaflet about how to stay healthy during flu season.
quote_zh: We handed out campaign leaflets for the school election, promising more pizza in the cafeteria.
ex_en: A single leaflet can change a mind; a single voice can start a movement.
ex_zh: Never underestimate the power of a leaflet—or the person who reads it.

def_en: On the side sheltered from the wind.
quote_en: We pitched our tent on the leeward side of the hill, where the wind couldn’t reach us.
quote_zh: The leeward slope of the mountain was green and calm, while the windward side was bare and rocky.
ex_en: Find your leeward—the place where storms can’t touch your peace.
ex_zh: A leeward heart is not weak; it knows where to anchor in the gale.

quote_en: Robin Hood is a legendary hero who stole from the rich to give to the poor.
quote_zh: The Loch Ness Monster is a legendary creature that people have searched for but never found.
ex_en: Legends are stories that live because they speak truth, even when the details blur.
ex_zh: You don’t need to be legendary to leave a legacy; a small kindness echoes.

quote_en: With a long pole, you can use leverage to lift a heavy rock that seems impossible alone.
quote_zh: In negotiations, having a good argument gives you leverage to get a fair deal.
ex_en: Leverage isn’t about pushing harder; it’s about pushing from the right place.
ex_zh: Use your strengths as leverage to lift others; that is true power.

quote_en: The queen’s royal lineage goes back hundreds of years, with portraits of every ancestor in the castle.
quote_zh: My family likes to trace lineage through old photos and stories handed down.
ex_en: Your lineage is not a chain; it’s a river that flows through you.
ex_zh: You carry your ancestors’ dreams; make them proud by being yourself.

quote_en: We bought planks of pine from the lumber yard to build a birdhouse.
quote_zh: The bear lumbered through the woods, shaking the ground with each heavy step.
ex_en: Lumber is the bones of a house; what you build with it becomes a home.
ex_zh: Sometimes you must lumber forward even when you feel clumsy; motion creates progress.

quote_en: We used a shiny fishing lure to catch trout in the lake, but the fish were smarter than us.
quote_zh: The lure of adventure made us climb the tree, even though Mom said not to.
ex_en: The brightest lure can hide a hook; look closely before you leap.
ex_zh: Be lured by what builds you up, not what only promises a moment’s shine.

def_zh: write a lyric poem; famous lyric poem
quote_en: For English class, we had to write a lyric poem about our favorite season—I chose autumn.
quote_zh: “The Road Not Taken” is a famous lyric poem about choices and the paths we take.
ex_en: A lyric poem is a window into a heart; write yours with honesty.
ex_zh: You don’t need to be a poet to live a lyric poem; your life can be the verse.

quote_en: We built a makeshift shelter from branches and leaves when the rain caught us on the trail.
quote_zh: During the sleepover, I used a sleeping bag on the floor as a makeshift bed.
ex_en: A makeshift solution can hold until something stronger is built—if you keep working.
ex_zh: Sometimes the best inventions start as makeshift; creativity loves constraint.

quote_en: A socially maladjusted child might struggle to make friends, but patience and guidance can help.
quote_zh: His maladjusted behavior in class—shouting out and not sharing—improved after he joined the art club.
ex_en: Maladjusted doesn’t mean broken; it means the environment isn’t right.
ex_zh: Find a place where you fit; sometimes you have to build it yourself.

quote_en: My uncle is a perpetual malcontent—he complains about the weather no matter how sunny it is.
quote_zh: The malcontent in the group kept grumbling, but the others cheered him up with a surprise cake.
ex_en: A malcontent sees what’s wrong; a builder sees what could be right.
ex_zh: Channel discontent into action; the world changes when the unhappy decide to help.

quote_en: The scientist studied a malformed leaf that grew in a spiral instead of flat.
quote_zh: The bird with a malformed wing couldn’t fly, but it hopped cheerfully around the sanctuary.
ex_en: Malformed does not mean worthless; it means different, and different can be beautiful.
ex_zh: Imperfections are not flaws; they are the marks of a unique story.

quote_en: A computer malfunction deleted my homework, but I had saved a backup.
quote_zh: The roller coaster stopped because of a mechanical malfunction, and we had to walk down the stairs.
ex_en: Every malfunction is a lesson; it shows you where the system needs care.
ex_zh: When life malfunctions, don’t panic—pause, breathe, and find the fix.

quote_en: Eating a variety of foods helps prevent malnutrition and keeps your body strong.
quote_zh: In some places, chronic malnutrition affects children’s growth, but feeding programs are making a difference.
ex_en: Malnutrition starves the body; lack of kindness starves the soul.
ex_zh: Feed yourself well—with food, with stories, with love.

quote_en: The doctor was sued for medical malpractice after making a serious mistake in surgery.
quote_zh: A lawyer who steals from clients is guilty of legal malpractice and loses their license.
ex_en: Malpractice is a betrayal of trust; trust is the foundation of every profession.
ex_zh: The best protection against malpractice is integrity—do the right thing even when no one watches.

quote_en: In the museum, we saw iron manacles used to chain prisoners hundreds of years ago.
quote_zh: Fear can be like manacles that hold you back from trying new things.
ex_en: Some manacles are made of metal; others are made of “what if.” Break free.
ex_zh: True freedom is not the absence of manacles, but the courage to choose your path.

quote_en: Learning to manage time means finishing homework before playing video games—then guilt‑free gaming.
quote_zh: Deep breathing and exercise help me manage stress before a big test.
ex_en: You cannot manage the wind, but you can manage your sails.
ex_zh: Manage your days well, and your days will manage you well.

def_en: Cosmetic care of the hands and nails.
quote_en: My mom treated herself to a manicure after a long week of work—it made her smile.
quote_zh: She keeps a small manicure set in her desk to fix a chipped nail in a minute.
ex_en: A manicure is a small act of self‑care; you deserve moments of beauty.
ex_zh: Tend to your hands as they tend to others; they are your instruments of kindness.

def_en: Relating to work done by hand.
quote_en: Our grandfather’s manual labor built the barn board by board; his hands told the story.
quote_zh: Manual skills like sewing and woodworking are useful and creative.
ex_en: Manual work is not lesser work; it is the work that makes the world solid.
ex_zh: There is dignity in manual labor; the hands that build are hands that bless.

def_en: To make by machine or hand.
quote_en: The factory manufactures toys that are shipped to stores all over the country.
quote_zh: In the detective story, the villain tried to manufacture evidence to frame an innocent person.
ex_en: To manufacture is to create; to create is to honor the gift of making.
ex_zh: Manufacture not only things, but also moments of joy, for they last longest.

quote_en: A single photo can be several megabytes of data, which adds up fast on your phone.
quote_zh: The game required two thousand megabytes to download; we waited an hour.
ex_en: A megabyte is a measure of information, but wisdom can’t be measured in megabytes.
ex_zh: In a world of bytes, remember to store the things that truly matter.

def_en: A large stone used in ancient monuments.
quote_en: Stonehenge is a circle of ancient megaliths that still puzzles archaeologists today.
quote_zh: The megalithic structure on the hill was probably a temple or burial ground for ancient people.
ex_en: A megalith whispers the names of those who dragged it into place; listen.
ex_zh: What you build may outlast you; make it worthy of the future.

quote_en: The coach used a megaphone to shout encouragement from the sidelines.
quote_zh: At the pep rally, we made our own megaphones from poster board to cheer louder.
ex_en: A megaphone makes a voice louder, but a kind word makes it heard.
ex_zh: You don’t need a megaphone to be heard; a quiet truth travels far.

def_en: The rhythmic pattern in poetry; a unit of length.
quote_en: Shakespeare wrote in iambic pentameter, a poetic meter that sounds like a heartbeat.
quote_zh: We put coins in the parking meter so we wouldn’t get a ticket while we shopped.
ex_en: Meter gives poetry its music; purpose gives life its rhythm.
ex_zh: Find your meter—the steady beat that keeps you moving forward.

quote_en: Yogurt contains beneficial microorganisms that help your digestion.
quote_zh: Washing your hands kills harmful microorganisms that can make you sick.
ex_en: Microorganisms remind us that even the invisible can have immense power.
ex_zh: The smallest life can teach the biggest lessons about interconnectedness.

def_en: An electromagnetic wave; an oven using such waves.
quote_en: A microwave oven heats food quickly, but it doesn’t make things crispy like a regular oven.
quote_zh: For movie night, we made microwave popcorn and sprinkled it with cheese powder.
ex_en: Speed is convenient, but some things—like friendship—shouldn’t be microwaved.
ex_zh: Let patience slow‑cook your dreams; they’ll taste better.

def_en: To move from one region to another.
quote_en: Every autumn, birds migrate south to escape the cold, flying thousands of miles.
quote_zh: My grandparents had to migrate to a new country for work, bringing their culture with them.
ex_en: To migrate is to carry home in your heart.
ex_zh: When you migrate to a new place, you plant seeds that will bloom for others.

quote_en: The old miser lived in a creaky house with piles of coins he never spent.
quote_zh: A miser hoarding wealth while others go hungry is a sad story—sharing brings joy.
ex_en: A miser dies with nothing; a giver dies with everything.
ex_zh: The richest people are not those with the most gold, but those with the most open hands.

def_en: A person sent to spread religious faith.
quote_en: My aunt did missionary work in Kenya, helping build a school and teaching English.
quote_zh: The missionary school taught children to read and write, opening doors they never had.
ex_en: A missionary carries a message; the best message is kindness in action.
ex_zh: You don’t need to travel to be a missionary; your life can be a mission of compassion.

def_en: To provide with a motive; to inspire.
quote_en: A good coach doesn’t just teach skills; they motivate others to believe in themselves.
quote_zh: I had to self‑motivate to practice piano when no one was watching.
ex_en: Motivate not with fear but with a vision of what you can become.
ex_zh: A single word can motivate a life; choose yours with care.

def_en: A story or account of events.
quote_en: For homework, we wrote a personal narrative about a time we overcame a fear.
quote_zh: After winning the championship, the team changed the narrative from “underdogs” to “champions.”
ex_en: The narrative you tell yourself shapes the life you live.
ex_zh: You are the author of your own narrative; write a story you’d be proud to read.

quote_en: My plan for the weekend was nebulous—maybe the park, maybe a movie, maybe nothing.
quote_zh: The memory of my great‑grandmother is nebulous, like a dream I can’t quite grasp.
ex_en: A nebulous dream needs definition; write it down and give it shape.
ex_zh: Even the nebulous can become clear with patient thought.

quote_en: A negligent driver who texts while driving puts everyone on the road in danger.
quote_zh: Negligent parenting can mean not knowing where your child is or who they’re with.
ex_en: Negligence is not a small mistake; it’s a choice to not care enough.
ex_zh: Be diligent in the small things; negligence grows from ignored cracks.

def_en: So small as to be unimportant.
quote_en: The extra sugar in my coffee is a negligible amount—it won’t change my health much.
quote_zh: His mistake had a negligible effect on the final grade, so he wasn’t worried.
ex_en: What seems negligible now may become significant later; tend to the small.
ex_zh: Kindness is never negligible; it adds up like pennies in a jar.

def_en: To discuss to reach an agreement.
quote_en: The two countries sent diplomats to negotiate a peace treaty after years of conflict.
quote_zh: We had to negotiate who got the front seat on the ride to school—rock, paper, scissors settled it.
ex_en: To negotiate is to find the win‑win; no one leaves angry.
ex_zh: Negotiate with yourself as well: find compromise between what you want and what you need.

quote_en: The pianist’s nimble fingers danced across the keys, playing a song we all recognized.
quote_zh: A nimble mind can solve puzzles and riddles that leave others stumped.
ex_en: Nimble doesn’t mean rushing; it means moving with ease and purpose.
ex_zh: Be nimble in thought, so you can adapt when life throws a curveball.

def_zh: title of nobility; nobility of character
quote_en: In some countries, a title of nobility is passed down through families, like duke or countess.
quote_zh: Her nobility of character shone when she gave her prize money to a sick classmate.
ex_en: True nobility is not in a title; it’s in how you treat those who have less.
ex_zh: The nobility of a soul is measured by the kindness it leaves behind.

quote_en: For creative writing, I decided to write a novelette about a girl who finds a hidden garden.
quote_zh: My grandmother loved reading romantic novelettes on the porch in the afternoons.
ex_en: A novelette is a small world; you can build one with a few pages.
ex_zh: Even a short story can change a life; brevity can hold depth.

def_zh: raise an objection; overrule an objection
quote_en: In court, the lawyer raised an objection when the other side asked an unfair question.
quote_zh: The judge overruled the objection and allowed the witness to answer.
ex_en: An objection can be a gift if it points to a truth you missed.
ex_zh: Raise your objection with respect; it’s more likely to be heard.

def_en: Annoyingly insensitive or slow to understand.
quote_en: His obtuse remark about her accent made everyone uncomfortable—he didn’t realize how it sounded.
quote_zh: Sometimes my brother is deliberately obtuse when asked to do chores, pretending not to hear.
ex_en: Obtuse can be a choice; choose instead to be curious and kind.
ex_zh: Sharpness of mind is good; sharpness of heart is better.

def_zh: onset of winter; onset of illness
quote_en: The onset of winter brought the first snow, and we built a snowman before breakfast.
quote_zh: At the onset of illness, rest is the best medicine to prevent it from getting worse.
ex_en: The onset of a journey is exciting; the journey itself is the reward.
ex_zh: Every great thing begins with an onset; be ready to take the first step.

quote_en: The school drama club performed a comic operetta about a mistaken‑identity mix‑up at a castle.
quote_zh: Gilbert and Sullivan wrote famous operettas like “The Pirates of Penzance” that are still sung today.
ex_en: An operetta reminds us that life can be both silly and serious, often at the same time.
ex_zh: Let your days be an operetta—mix laughter with melody, and sing your part.

def_en: Derived from living matter; natural development.
quote_en: We buy organic food from the farmers’ market because it’s grown without synthetic pesticides.
quote_zh: The neighborhood’s renewal was organic—people started gardens, then a community center, then a festival.
ex_en: Organic growth takes time, but its roots go deep.
ex_zh: The best solutions are organic; they grow from the ground of real need.

quote_en: The bake sale got an overwhelming response; we sold out in the first hour.
quote_zh: Winning the championship brought an overwhelming emotion of joy and relief.
ex_en: What feels overwhelming today may feel like a small step tomorrow.
ex_zh: When overwhelmed, breathe; divide the mountain into pebbles.

def_en: Side by side and never meeting; similar.
quote_en: In geometry, parallel lines run side by side but never touch, like train tracks.
quote_zh: The two sisters led parallel lives, both becoming teachers in different towns.
ex_en: Parallel paths can still be journeys together; you don’t have to merge to be close.
ex_zh: Sometimes the best support is parallel—running alongside, not ahead.

quote_en: The parched earth cracked under the summer sun, begging for rain.
quote_zh: After running the mile, my throat was parched, and I gulped a whole bottle of water.
ex_en: A parched land waits for rain; a parched heart waits for kindness.
ex_zh: Quench the parched with simple gifts: water, shade, a listening ear.

quote_en: We need a valid passport to travel abroad; my picture looked nothing like me.
quote_zh: For the passport photo, you have to look straight ahead without smiling—it’s the hardest smile not to smile.
ex_en: A passport opens borders; a kind heart opens doors.
ex_zh: Your true passport is your character; it travels everywhere with you.

def_en: A poor farmer of low social status.
quote_en: In medieval times, a peasant worked the land for the lord in exchange for protection.
quote_zh: The museum exhibit showed peasant life—simple homes, wooden tools, and woven cloth.
ex_en: A peasant may have little, but can be rich in wisdom and love.
ex_zh: Dignity is not in titles; it’s in how you live, not what you own.

def_en: A weight that swings back and forth.
def_zh: swing like a pendulum; pendulum clock
quote_en: The grandfather clock’s pendulum swung slowly, marking each second with a steady tick.
quote_zh: The argument swung like a pendulum—first one side winning, then the other.
ex_en: The pendulum teaches that extremes always return to center.
ex_zh: When life swings too far, remember the pendulum always comes back.

def_en: Land surrounded by water on three sides.
quote_en: Florida is a peninsula, with the Atlantic Ocean on one side and the Gulf of Mexico on the other.
quote_zh: The Korean Peninsula is divided into two countries, but the land itself is one.
ex_en: A peninsula connects to the mainland but reaches into the sea; you can be both grounded and adventurous.
ex_zh: Don’t be afraid to be a peninsula—stand firm yet open to new horizons.

quote_en: My grandfather collects a pension from the factory where he worked for forty years.
quote_zh: Some jobs offer a pension plan so that workers can retire with security.
ex_en: A pension is a reward for a lifetime of work; it says “we remember.”
ex_zh: The greatest pension is not money, but the respect of those you served.

def_en: To become aware of through the senses.
quote_en: Dogs can perceive a change in the weather before humans, sometimes acting anxious before a storm.
quote_zh: A lifeguard must perceive danger quickly to prevent accidents in the water.
ex_en: To perceive is to see beyond the obvious—to notice what others miss.
ex_zh: The heart perceives what the eyes cannot; listen to it.

def_en: The ability to see or understand.
quote_en: Reading books about other cultures can change your perception of people who seem different.
quote_zh: We tested depth perception by trying to touch our fingertips together with one eye closed.
ex_en: Perception is not reality; reality is what you do with what you perceive.
ex_zh: Your perception shapes your world; polish the lens with curiosity.

quote_en: The flagpole stands perpendicular to the ground, straight and true.
quote_zh: In the room, the bookshelf was perpendicular to the window, creating a cozy reading nook.
ex_en: Stand perpendicular to injustice—upright and unyielding.
ex_zh: A perpendicular life is one that doesn’t lean with every wind.

quote_en: Everyone needs personal space; crowding someone can make them uncomfortable.
quote_zh: Keep your personal belongings in your locker so they don’t get lost.
ex_en: Personal growth is a private journey, but it’s okay to invite others along.
ex_zh: Your personal story is yours; share it when you trust, guard it when you must.

quote_en: Military personnel serve their country in many roles, from nurses to pilots.
quote_zh: All school personnel—teachers, custodians, office staff—work to make learning possible.
ex_en: Personnel are the heart of any organization; treat them well, and the organization thrives.
ex_zh: Behind every service is a person; honor the personnel who make life run.

quote_en: Farmers spray pesticide to protect crops from insects, but they must be careful not to harm bees.
quote_zh: Washing fruits and vegetables removes pesticide residue that can be left on the skin.
ex_en: The best pesticide is a balanced ecosystem where pests are kept in check naturally.
ex_zh: Choose solutions that don’t poison the very things you aim to protect.

quote_en: In health class, we learned about human physiology—how the heart pumps and lungs breathe.
quote_zh: Exercise physiology studies how the body changes and strengthens during physical activity.
ex_en: Physiology teaches that every part of you matters; treat your body with respect.
ex_zh: The more you know your physiology, the better you can care for the vessel that carries you.

quote_en: My grandmother’s wedding ring is platinum, a metal that never tarnishes.
quote_zh: When an album sells a million copies, it’s called platinum record—a huge achievement.
ex_en: Platinum is rare, but kindness is rarer still; both are precious.
ex_zh: You are worth more than platinum; never let anyone tell you otherwise.

def_zh: ponder a question; ponder a decision
quote_en: I had to ponder the question of whether honesty always means telling everything.
quote_zh: Before choosing a high school, I will ponder the decision with my family.
ex_en: Ponder not to stall, but to understand more deeply.
ex_zh: The answers you seek are often found in the space you give yourself to ponder.

quote_en: At the train station, a porter helped us carry our heavy suitcases to the platform.
quote_zh: The hotel porter greeted us with a smile and showed us to our room.
ex_en: A porter bears the weight of others; their work is a kind of grace.
ex_zh: Everyone needs a porter sometimes—someone to share the load.

quote_en: In the school play, I had to portray a nervous astronaut about to launch into space.
quote_zh: The actor portrayed fear so convincingly that the audience gasped.
ex_en: To portray someone is to step into their shoes; empathy is the greatest acting.
ex_zh: Your life will portray your values; make it a picture worth framing.

def_en: The state of being extremely poor.
quote_en: Many families live in poverty, struggling to afford food and shelter.
quote_zh: The poverty line is the minimum income needed to meet basic needs.
ex_en: Poverty is not a character flaw; it is a condition that can be changed.
ex_zh: The greatest poverty is not of money, but of hope; give hope and you give everything.

quote_en: A practical solution to the messy room was a set of labeled bins for toys, books, and art supplies.
quote_zh: Learning practical skills like cooking and sewing prepares you for independence.
ex_en: Practical wisdom is knowing what works, not just what sounds good.
ex_zh: Be practical in your plans, but never let practicality smother your dreams.

def_en: A large area of flat grassland.
quote_en: The prairie stretched for miles, the grass waving like a golden ocean.
quote_zh: Prairie dogs live in underground towns, popping up to chirp warnings.
ex_en: The prairie teaches that beauty can be vast and quiet; you don’t need mountains to be majestic.
ex_zh: In the prairie of life, find your place and let your roots run deep.

quote_en: The stack of books was in a precarious position—one sneeze and it would topple.
quote_zh: The hiker stood on a precarious ledge, testing his balance before moving forward.
ex_en: Life can feel precarious, but you are steadier than you know.
ex_zh: Build your foundations strong, so when the ground shifts, you still stand.

quote_en: Some breads contain a food preservative that keeps them soft for days, but fresh bread is best.
quote_zh: We painted the fence with a wood preservative to protect it from rain and rot.
ex_en: The best preservative for memories is sharing them with others.
ex_zh: Preserve what matters—not things, but relationships and values.

def_en: To suppose; to take for granted.
quote_en: In court, the law presumes innocence until guilt is proven—everyone gets a fair chance.
quote_zh: It’s rude to presume to know what someone else is thinking without asking.
ex_en: Presume good intentions; you’ll often find them.
ex_zh: Don’t presume failure before you try; you might surprise yourself.

quote_en: A presumption of guilt before hearing the facts is unfair and unjust.
quote_zh: His false presumption that he would win made the loss even harder.
ex_en: Presumption closes doors; curiosity opens them.
ex_zh: Lay aside presumption and ask; the answers may change everything.

def_en: An established way of doing something.
quote_en: Before the fire drill, we reviewed the safety procedure: line up, walk quietly, meet on the field.
quote_zh: The voting procedure for class president was explained so everyone knew how to cast their ballot.
ex_en: A good procedure makes hard things easier; it’s a path worn smooth.
ex_zh: Follow the procedure, but never be afraid to improve it.

def_en: To poke; to urge into action.
def_zh: prod with a stick; prod someone into action
quote_en: We used a long stick to prod the turtle off the road, moving it to safety.
quote_zh: The coach’s pep talk prodded the team into action; they played with new energy.
ex_en: A gentle prod can be kindness; a harsh one, cruelty.
ex_zh: Prod yourself gently; growth comes from nudge, not shove.

quote_en: The progressive school focused on projects and creativity instead of just tests.
quote_zh: A progressive tax takes a larger percentage from those who can afford to pay more.
ex_en: Progressive ideas move us forward, but they need roots in what works.
ex_zh: Be progressive in your thinking, but keep your feet on the ground.

quote_en: In physics class, we launched a projectile from a catapult and measured how far it flew.
quote_zh: The knight fired a projectile from the castle wall—a flaming arrow to signal allies.
ex_en: Words can be projectiles; aim them to build, not to wound.
ex_zh: The trajectory of a projectile teaches that force and angle matter; so does intention.

quote_en: The weather projection says rain on Saturday, so we moved the picnic to Sunday.
quote_zh: The movie projection flickered at first, then the film came in clear.
ex_en: Projection of fear can create what we fear; choose to project hope instead.
ex_zh: Your projections of the future are just guesses; keep them open to surprise.

quote_en: My dad made a business proposition to open a food truck selling tacos.
quote_zh: Jumping off the diving board was a risky proposition for someone afraid of heights.
ex_en: Every proposition is an invitation; you can say yes, no, or maybe.
ex_zh: The best propositions are those where everyone leaves better than they came.

quote_en: The town enjoyed economic prosperity after the new factory brought jobs.
quote_zh: In the story, prosperity and peace came when the king learned to share with his people.
ex_en: True prosperity is not just having more; it’s needing less and sharing what you have.
ex_zh: Prosperity without generosity is a hollow bell.

quote_en: Beans, eggs, and fish are good protein sources for building strong muscles.
quote_zh: After the workout, I drank a protein shake to help my body recover.
ex_en: Protein builds the body; love builds the spirit.
ex_zh: Fuel yourself well, so you have energy to fuel others.

quote_en: The teasing was meant to provoke a reaction, but she ignored it and walked away.
quote_zh: Bullies try to provoke anger so they can blame you for fighting back.
ex_en: Provoke thought, not hurt; challenge ideas, not people.
ex_zh: A kind word can provoke a smile; a cruel one, a scar.

quote_en: In child psychology, we learn how kids grow and think at different ages.
quote_zh: Sports psychology helps athletes stay focused and calm under pressure.
ex_en: Psychology shows us that our minds are powerful; we can learn to steer them.
ex_zh: Understanding psychology is understanding yourself; that’s the first step to growth.

quote_en: The invention of the internet was a radical change in how people share information.
quote_zh: Her radical idea to start a school garden seemed crazy at first, but now we have fresh vegetables.
ex_en: Radical solutions often come from asking “what if?”—the most powerful question.
ex_zh: Don’t be afraid of radical kindness; it can change the world.

def_zh: random number; random act of kindness
quote_en: The computer generated a random number between one and ten for our lottery.
quote_zh: A random act of kindness, like paying for a stranger’s coffee, can make someone’s day.
ex_en: Random doesn’t mean meaningless; sometimes chance brings the best gifts.
ex_zh: Sprinkle random kindness; you never know whose heart it will touch.

quote_en: Buying the expensive sneakers was a rash decision—I didn’t have enough money left for lunch.
quote_zh: He made a rash promise to stay up all night, but fell asleep by ten.
ex_en: A rash choice is like a pebble thrown in water; you can’t take back the ripples.
ex_zh: Pause before you act; a moment’s thought can save a world of regret.

quote_en: The painting combined magical realism—floating umbrellas—with everyday street scenes.
quote_zh: Political realism means understanding that nations act in their own interest.
ex_en: Realism is not cynicism; it’s seeing clearly without losing hope.
ex_zh: Balance realism with dreams; eyes open, heart forward.

quote_en: My aunt reared three children on her own, with help from grandparents.
quote_zh: On the farm, they rear livestock—cows, pigs, and chickens.
ex_en: To rear something is to nurture it from small to strong.
ex_zh: You may not rear children yet, but you can rear ideas, friendships, and dreams.

quote_en: The store offered a mail-in rebate: buy the tablet, send a form, get $20 back.
quote_zh: After filing taxes, my parents got a tax rebate that paid for summer camp.
ex_en: A rebate is a welcome surprise; but the best returns come from investing in others.
ex_zh: Sometimes life gives you a rebate of hope when you least expect it.

quote_en: In the debate, her strong rebuttal used facts and examples that won over the audience.
quote_zh: The lawyer’s legal rebuttal dismantled the other side’s argument point by point.
ex_en: A good rebuttal is not angry; it’s calm and rooted in truth.
ex_zh: The best rebuttal to hate is to live a life of kindness.

quote_en: The worker sought financial recompense for the hours he worked but wasn’t paid.
quote_zh: No recompense could match the joy of seeing the garden we planted bloom.
ex_en: The greatest recompense is knowing you made a difference.
ex_zh: Don’t work only for recompense; work for the work itself.

def_en: To compensate; to save from sin; to exchange for something better.
quote_en: At the arcade, you can redeem your tickets for a stuffed animal or a yo-yo.
quote_zh: After making a mistake, you can redeem yourself by apologizing and doing better next time.
ex_en: To redeem is to turn failure into a lesson, and a lesson into a step forward.
ex_zh: Every day offers a chance to redeem a small corner of the world with kindness.

quote_en: The movie was a story of redemption—a villain who becomes a hero by saving others.
quote_zh: After the loss, the team sought redemption in the championship game and won.
ex_en: Redemption is not a single moment; it’s a path walked with honest steps.
ex_zh: You don’t need to be perfect to deserve redemption; you need only to try again.

def_en: Unnecessarily repetitive; exceeding what is needed.
quote_en: The essay had redundant information—the same point repeated three times in different words.
quote_zh: When the factory automated, some workers became redundant, but the company helped them retrain.
ex_en: Redundant words clutter; redundant kindness never does.
ex_zh: In life, it’s better to be redundant in love than stingy with it.

def_en: To abstain; to hold back; n. a repeated line in a song.
quote_en: During the test, please refrain from talking so everyone can concentrate.
quote_zh: The song’s refrain—“We shall overcome”—was sung over and over, growing louder each time.
ex_en: To refrain from anger is not weakness; it’s choosing to let peace have the last word.
ex_zh: Sometimes the most powerful action is to refrain and listen.

def_zh: reimburse expenses; reimburse for a purchase
quote_en: The school will reimburse your expenses for the field trip if you keep all your receipts.
quote_zh: I lent my friend five dollars for lunch, and she reimbursed me the next day.
ex_en: To reimburse a debt of the heart is impossible; you pass it forward.
ex_zh: The best reimbursement for kindness is seeing someone smile because of you.

def_zh: reject an offer; reject a hypothesis
quote_en: I had to reject the offer of extra dessert because I was already stuffed.
quote_zh: In science, you might reject a hypothesis if the experiment doesn’t support it.
ex_en: To reject a dream is to close a door before you’ve seen what’s behind it.
ex_zh: Reject what diminishes you; embrace what makes you grow.

def_en: A return to a former state, especially after improvement.
def_zh: relapse into bad habits; prevent a relapse
quote_en: After weeks of eating healthy, he had a relapse into junk food over the weekend.
quote_zh: Taking your medicine as prescribed helps prevent a relapse of the illness.
ex_en: A relapse is not a failure; it’s a reminder that healing is not a straight line.
ex_zh: If you relapse, start again; persistence is the real victory.

def_zh: reminisce about childhood; reminisce with old friends
quote_en: At the reunion, the cousins reminisced about summer trips to the lake.
quote_zh: My grandmother loves to reminisce about her first job at the bakery, kneading dough at dawn.
ex_en: To reminisce is to keep the past alive in the present, like a fire that warms the heart.
ex_zh: The best memories are those you reminisce with others; they grow sweeter in sharing.

def_zh: be remiss in duty; remiss of me
quote_en: I was remiss in returning the library book, so I paid a small fine.
quote_zh: It would be remiss of me not to thank my teacher for all her help.
ex_en: To be remiss is human; to correct it is wise.
ex_zh: Don’t be remiss with your words of gratitude; they matter more than you know.

quote_en: The team had to renegotiate the contract when the original stadium plans changed.
quote_zh: We renegotiated the terms of our club: meetings moved to Fridays so more could attend.
ex_en: To renegotiate is to adapt; the wise change course when the river bends.
ex_zh: Relationships, like agreements, sometimes need to be renegotiated with honesty.

def_zh: renovate a house; renovate a park
quote_en: My family plans to renovate the kitchen, knocking down a wall to make it bigger.
quote_zh: Volunteers came together to renovate the old park, adding benches and a new swing set.
ex_en: To renovate is to honor what was while making it fit for what will be.
ex_zh: You can renovate your own habits, one small improvement at a time.

quote_en: Citronella candles help repel mosquitoes on summer nights.
quote_zh: The castle walls were built to repel invaders with high stone and boiling oil.
ex_en: Some people repel kindness because they’ve never felt it; be patient.
ex_zh: The things that repel you may be the very things you need to understand.

quote_en: I received a reprimand for running in the hallway, and I promised to be more careful.
quote_zh: The teacher’s stern reprimand made the class quiet down and listen.
ex_en: A reprimand given with care can guide; given with cruelty, it wounds.
ex_zh: Accept a reprimand as a mirror; it shows where you can polish yourself.

def_zh: reside in a city; reside within
quote_en: My grandparents reside in a small town where everyone knows your name.
quote_zh: The power to forgive resides within each of us, waiting to be used.
ex_en: Where you reside is less important than what resides in your heart.
ex_zh: The truest home is the love that resides in the people around you.

quote_en: She showed great resolve by practicing piano every day until she mastered the difficult piece.
quote_zh: The principal helped resolve the dispute by listening to both sides calmly.
ex_en: Resolve is not about never falling; it’s about rising each time you do.
ex_zh: The hardest problems are resolved not with force, but with patience and understanding.

def_zh: revise an essay; revise a plan
quote_en: After my teacher’s comments, I revised my essay to make the ending stronger.
quote_zh: We had to revise our plan for the science fair when our first experiment failed.
ex_en: To revise is to grow; every draft can be better than the last.
ex_zh: Life itself is a revision; don’t be afraid to rewrite your story.

quote_en: “Cat” and “hat” are rhyming words—they share the same ending sound.
quote_zh: My favorite nursery rhyme as a child was “Hey Diddle Diddle, the cat and the fiddle.”
ex_en: Rhyme gives poetry its music; kindness gives life its melody.
ex_zh: May your days rhyme with joy, even if the tune is sometimes slow.

quote_en: My morning ritual: brush teeth, make bed, eat cereal while watching cartoons.
quote_zh: Every Friday night, our family ritual is pizza and a movie together.
ex_en: Rituals anchor us; they remind us what we value.
ex_zh: The small rituals of kindness—a hug, a note, a shared meal—weave a life of meaning.

def_en: A competitive exhibition of cowboy skills.
quote_en: For my birthday, my dad took me to attend a rodeo with bucking broncos and barrel racing.
quote_zh: The rodeo clown distracted the bull so the rider could escape safely.
ex_en: Life can feel like a rodeo—wild and unpredictable. Hang on and stay true.
ex_zh: The real rodeo is inside; tame your fears and ride with courage.

quote_en: A pipeline rupture caused an oil spill, and volunteers worked to clean the shore.
quote_zh: A careless rumor caused a rupture in their friendship, but time and honesty mended it.
ex_en: Some ruptures are necessary; they let old things fall away to make room for new.
ex_zh: A rupture, properly healed, can become the strongest place.

def_en: The use of humor to criticize folly.
quote_en: The cartoon was political satire, poking fun at both parties with clever drawings.
quote_zh: The author’s sharp satire made readers laugh while thinking about serious issues.
ex_en: Satire holds a mirror to society; laughter can open doors that anger keeps locked.
ex_zh: The best satire makes you smile, then think, then want to make things better.

def_zh: saturate with water; saturate the market
quote_en: Rain saturated the ground so much that puddles formed in every dip.
quote_zh: New phone models saturate the market each year, making old ones obsolete.
ex_en: Saturate your mind with good books; they will shape your thoughts like water shapes stone.
ex_zh: A heart saturated with kindness cannot be hardened by unkindness.

quote_en: In some places, water scarcity means families walk miles for clean drinking water.
quote_zh: During the drought, there was a scarcity of fresh vegetables at the market.
ex_en: Scarcity teaches us to value what we have and to share with those who have less.
ex_zh: The scarcity of time is the best reminder to spend it on what truly matters.

quote_en: In a fire drill, we practice the scenario of a real fire so we know what to do.
quote_zh: The worst-case scenario was that it would rain on field day, so we planned indoor games.
ex_en: A scenario is a story of what could be; you are the author of what actually is.
ex_zh: Think through scenarios, but don’t let fear of the worst keep you from the best.

quote_en: We chose a color scheme of blue and green for the mural, matching the ocean theme.
quote_zh: In the story, the villain’s scheme was to steal the crown, but the hero foiled it.
ex_en: A scheme without goodness is a house without a foundation; it will fall.
ex_zh: The best schemes are the ones where everyone wins.

def_en: To run hurriedly; to deliberately cause failure.
def_zh: scuttle across the floor; scuttle a plan
quote_en: A crab scuttled across the sand, disappearing into a hole before we could catch it.
quote_zh: The spy tried to scuttle the plan by leaking secrets, but the team had a backup.
ex_en: Don’t scuttle your own dreams with fear; let them sail.
ex_zh: Sometimes you must scuttle old ideas to make room for better ones.

quote_en: We found a secluded beach where the only footprints were our own.
quote_zh: The writer rented a secluded cabin in the woods to finish her novel.
ex_en: A secluded place can be a gift—a space to hear your own thoughts.
ex_zh: Even in a secluded spot, you are connected to the world by your heart.

def_en: An agricultural laborer bound to the land.
quote_en: In medieval Europe, a serf worked the lord’s land and couldn’t leave without permission.
quote_zh: The serf system ended when people demanded freedom and fair treatment.
ex_en: A serf may be bound to land, but a free spirit cannot be bound.
ex_zh: No one should be a serf to fear or poverty; we can help each other be free.

quote_en: Some colonists came as indentured servants, working for several years to pay for their passage.
quote_zh: Forced servitude is a crime against humanity; freedom is a right for all.
ex_en: Servitude of the body can be imposed; servitude of the spirit is chosen.
ex_zh: The truest freedom is not the absence of chains, but the presence of hope.

def_zh: severity of the punishment; severity of the storm
quote_en: The severity of the punishment surprised everyone; a week of detention for a small mistake.
quote_zh: The severity of the storm forced families to evacuate their homes.
ex_en: Severity can teach, but kindness teaches longer.
ex_zh: The severity of a challenge is not a measure of your strength; your response is.

quote_en: We saw a shoal of silver fish darting through the clear water.
quote_zh: The ship ran aground on a hidden shoal that wasn’t marked on the map.
ex_en: A shoal can be a danger or a nursery for young fish; it’s all about knowing the waters.
ex_zh: Life’s shoals are the challenges that make you steer with more care.

def_zh: family shrine; shrine to a saint
quote_en: In many homes, a family shrine holds photos of ancestors and candles for remembrance.
quote_zh: Pilgrims travel miles to visit the shrine of a saint they honor.
ex_en: A shrine is a place where the ordinary touches the extraordinary; find yours.
ex_zh: The heart can be a shrine; keep it filled with what you hold sacred.

quote_en: The farm’s grain silo stood tall against the sky, filled with corn from the autumn harvest.
quote_zh: In a company, an information silo happens when teams don’t share what they know.
ex_en: A silo may protect grain, but sharing it makes a community.
ex_zh: Break down the silos in your life; let ideas and kindness flow between them.

quote_en: A sinister figure in a hooded cloak appeared at the edge of the forest.
quote_zh: The detective uncovered a sinister plot to steal the crown jewels.
ex_en: A sinister act is often born from a heart that has forgotten its own worth.
ex_zh: Do not be fooled by sinister appearances; sometimes the scariest things are only shadows.

quote_en: Slothful habits like sleeping in and skipping breakfast can make mornings stressful.
quote_zh: The slothful worker was often found napping when he should have been building.
ex_en: Slothful is not resting; it’s avoiding what you know you should do.
ex_zh: Rest is good; sloth steals your potential.

quote_en: In sociology class, we learned how neighborhoods shape the people who grow up in them.
quote_zh: She wants to study sociology to understand poverty and how to reduce it.
ex_en: Sociology teaches that no one is an island; we rise and fall together.
ex_zh: The greatest lesson of sociology is that kindness builds stronger communities than walls.

quote_en: Her spendthrift habits left her with no savings, even though she earned a good salary.
quote_zh: The lottery winner turned out to be a notorious spendthrift, bankrupt within two years.
ex_en: A spendthrift spends without thought; a wise spender invests in what matters.
ex_zh: The opposite of a spendthrift is not a miser, but a steward who uses resources well.

quote_en: The earth is a sphere, slightly flattened at the poles.
quote_zh: A king’s sphere of influence extended to neighboring lands through treaties and alliances.
ex_en: Your sphere of influence begins with your own actions; they ripple outward.
ex_zh: Be a positive sphere—your own center of gravity that draws goodness toward you.

quote_en: In ancient times, the spoils of war included gold, cattle, and captured people.
quote_zh: The spoils system gave government jobs to supporters, not necessarily the most qualified.
ex_en: The spoils of victory are hollow if you’ve lost your honor to get them.
ex_zh: True spoils are not taken; they are earned through fair effort.

quote_en: The children performed a sprightly dance at the festival, spinning and hopping with joy.
quote_zh: My neighbor is a sprightly old man who still jogs every morning at eighty.
ex_en: A sprightly spirit is not about age; it’s about a heart that hasn’t stopped playing.
ex_zh: Keep your soul sprightly; it’s the secret to staying young in spirit.

def_en: The right side of a ship.
quote_en: The captain shouted, “Starboard side!” as a wave crashed over the right rail.
quote_zh: To avoid the rocks, the helmsman turned to starboard, pointing the bow away from danger.
ex_en: A good captain knows starboard from port; a good person knows right from wrong.
ex_zh: Let your moral compass be as clear as the starboard side of a ship.

quote_en: Corn starch is used to thicken sauces and gravies in cooking.
quote_zh: Potatoes have a lot of starch, which gives them their fluffy texture when baked.
ex_en: Starch gives fabric its crispness; integrity gives character its strength.
ex_zh: Don’t let the starch of pride make you too stiff to bend with grace.

quote_en: The museum displayed a bronze statuette of a horse from ancient Greece.
quote_zh: The Oscar is a famous award statuette given to actors and filmmakers.
ex_en: A statuette can represent achievement, but the real award is the work you did to earn it.
ex_zh: Some treasures are small—like a statuette—but they carry large meaning.

def_en: Strict; severe; the rear of a ship.
quote_en: My math teacher is stern but fair; she expects our best and helps us reach it.
quote_zh: The lifeguard gave a stern warning about running near the pool edge.
ex_en: A stern word can guide; a kind follow‑up makes it stick.
ex_zh: The stern of the ship faces the wake; sometimes you must look back to move forward.

def_zh: straddle a fence; straddle two cultures
quote_en: I tried to straddle the fence between two sides of the argument, but everyone wanted me to choose.
quote_zh: Children of immigrants often straddle two cultures, blending traditions from both.
ex_en: To straddle is to be a bridge; it’s not always comfortable, but it’s powerful.
ex_zh: You can straddle different worlds and build connections that others miss.

def_en: A narrow passage of water; difficulty.
quote_en: The Bering Strait separates Alaska from Russia, only about 50 miles wide.
quote_zh: After losing his job, the family was in dire straits, but neighbors helped them through.
ex_en: A strait can be a dangerous passage or a path to new waters.
ex_zh: In dire straits, you discover who your true friends are.

quote_en: The general’s stratagem involved faking a retreat to lure the enemy into a trap.
quote_zh: Using a clever stratagem, the younger sister convinced her brother to trade his cookie for a carrot.
ex_en: A stratagem may win a battle, but honesty wins the war of trust.
ex_zh: The best stratagem is to be so kind that no one wants to oppose you.

def_en: A topic; a person under authority.
def_zh: subject of the essay; test subject
quote_en: The subject of my essay was “How to Save the Bees” after learning about their importance.
quote_zh: In the science experiment, the test subject was a plant grown under different lights.
ex_en: You are the subject of your own life story; write it with courage.
ex_zh: No one should be a subject of unfair treatment; we are all equal in dignity.

quote_en: The first test was hard, but subsequent events showed we had learned from our mistakes.
quote_zh: The founders wrote the Constitution for subsequent generations to amend and improve.
ex_en: Subsequent generations inherit our choices; let’s leave them a world of kindness.
ex_zh: The consequences of today’s actions will be felt by those subsequent; choose wisely.

quote_en: In subsistence farming, families grow only enough to feed themselves, with little extra to sell.
quote_zh: Many people around the world live at subsistence level, struggling to meet basic needs.
ex_en: Subsistence is surviving; thriving is when you have enough to share.
ex_zh: From subsistence, the human spirit can still rise to create art, music, and love.

quote_en: At the pool, lifeguards supervise children to keep them safe.
quote_zh: The teacher supervised our science fair project, guiding us but letting us do the work.
ex_en: To supervise is to serve; the best leaders are the best helpers.
ex_zh: You may supervise others, but first learn to supervise yourself.

quote_en: My supportive family cheered me on at every soccer game, even when we lost.
quote_zh: A supportive environment at school helps students feel safe to ask questions.
ex_en: Supportive words are like sunshine; they help things grow.
ex_zh: Be supportive even when you don’t fully understand; sometimes presence is enough.

quote_en: I can only surmise the outcome of the election; the polls are too close to call.
quote_zh: Without evidence, we can merely surmise what happened; we need facts.
ex_en: Surmise is a starting point, not a conclusion; seek truth beyond your guesses.
ex_zh: Don’t let surmise become assumption; ask, listen, learn.

quote_en: When watching a fantasy movie, you have to suspend disbelief to enjoy the magic.
quote_zh: The school may suspend a student for serious rule-breaking while they investigate.
ex_en: Sometimes you must suspend judgment until you know the full story.
ex_zh: Suspending anger allows peace to enter.

quote_en: Modern technology lets us video chat with relatives on the other side of the world.
quote_zh: Assistive technology helps people with disabilities communicate and move more easily.
ex_en: Technology is a tool; what matters is how we use it—for connection or isolation.
ex_zh: The greatest technology is the human heart; it runs on kindness.

quote_en: Medical terminology can be confusing; “myocardial infarction” means heart attack.
quote_zh: Legal terminology often uses Latin phrases that take years to learn.
ex_en: Terminology can open doors or build walls; use it to include, not exclude.
ex_zh: Learn the terminology of kindness; it’s spoken in every language.

quote_en: Some people study theology to become priests or rabbis, exploring questions of faith.
quote_zh: Liberation theology focuses on helping the poor and oppressed, linking faith with justice.
ex_en: Theology asks the deepest questions: why we are here, how we should live.
ex_zh: You don’t need a degree in theology to live a life of compassion.

quote_en: A thrifty shopper compares prices and uses coupons to save money.
quote_zh: Thrifty habits like turning off lights and fixing things instead of replacing them help the planet.
ex_en: Thrifty is not cheap; it’s using resources wisely so you can be generous.
ex_zh: Be thrifty with money, lavish with love.

quote_en: Factories must be careful not to release environmental toxins into rivers and air.
quote_zh: Some bacteria produce a toxin in spoiled food that can make you very sick.
ex_en: Toxins can poison the body; hatred poisons the soul.
ex_zh: The best antidote to a toxin of spirit is a dose of kindness.

quote_en: For Thanksgiving, my family cooks traditional foods like turkey, stuffing, and pumpkin pie.
quote_zh: The school’s traditional dance is held every spring, with students learning folk steps from their ancestors.
ex_en: Traditional wisdom holds truths that new fashions often forget.
ex_zh: Honor traditions, but also be brave enough to start new ones.

def_en: The path of a moving object.
def_zh: trajectory of a rocket; career trajectory
quote_en: The trajectory of the rocket curved toward the moon, right on course.
quote_zh: Her career trajectory went from intern to CEO in just ten years of hard work.
ex_en: Your trajectory is shaped by the choices you make; aim high.
ex_zh: Even a small change in direction early on can alter a whole trajectory.

def_en: Change from one state to another.
def_zh: smooth transition; transition to middle school
quote_en: A good principal plans for a smooth transition when a new teacher arrives.
quote_zh: The transition to middle school felt scary at first, but soon we made new friends.
ex_en: Transition is a bridge between what was and what will be; cross it with courage.
ex_zh: In every transition, you carry the best of your past into your future.

quote_en: A small earth tremor shook the house for a few seconds—no damage, but we all felt it.
quote_zh: A tremor of fear ran through me before the big speech, but I took a breath and began.
ex_en: The smallest tremor can be a warning; pay attention to the quiet signs.
ex_zh: A tremor of hope can be the beginning of change.

quote_en: My ultimate goal is to become a veterinarian and help animals.
quote_zh: Soldiers who die for their country make the ultimate sacrifice for our freedom.
ex_en: The ultimate victory is not over others, but over your own fears.
ex_zh: Your ultimate worth is not in what you have, but in what you give.

quote_en: Her unaccountable behavior—laughing for no reason—made us wonder if she knew a secret.
quote_zh: The unaccountable delay in the train arrival frustrated everyone.
ex_en: Power without accountability is dangerous; hold yourself answerable.
ex_zh: Some mysteries are unaccountable; that’s what makes life an adventure.

def_zh: unaccustomed to the cold; unaccustomed luxury
quote_en: After living in Florida, I was unaccustomed to the cold when we moved north.
quote_zh: The farmer felt unaccustomed to the luxury of the city hotel.
ex_en: What is unaccustomed can feel uncomfortable at first; give it time.
ex_zh: Growth happens in the unaccustomed space beyond your comfort zone.

quote_en: The teacher gave an unambiguous answer: “Yes, the test is tomorrow.”
quote_zh: The red flag was an unambiguous signal to stop the race.
ex_en: Unambiguous honesty builds trust; ambiguity can hide, but it cannot build.
ex_zh: Be unambiguous in your kindness; let there be no doubt of your care.

quote_en: A good referee gives an unbiased opinion, calling fouls on both teams equally.
quote_zh: The contest needed an unbiased judge who didn’t know any of the entrants.
ex_en: Unbiased doesn’t mean without values; it means weighing facts without favor.
ex_zh: Strive to be unbiased in your judgments, but passionate in your kindness.

quote_en: Burping at the dinner table is considered uncouth behavior in many cultures.
quote_zh: His uncouth manners—interrupting and talking with his mouth full—embarrassed his family.
ex_en: Uncouth can be unlearned; manners are simply kindness in motion.
ex_zh: The uncouth exterior may hide a kind heart; give people a chance to show it.

quote_en: In the movie, the villain’s loyal underling did all the dirty work.
quote_zh: The boss treated every underling with respect, knowing the team worked best together.
ex_en: An underling is not lesser; they are part of the whole.
ex_zh: Treat every underling as you would want to be treated; you may be one someday.

quote_en: The ocean’s unfathomable depths hold creatures we’ve never seen.
quote_zh: The meaning of life is an unfathomable mystery; perhaps the search itself is the answer.
ex_en: Some things are unfathomable, but that doesn’t mean we stop trying to understand.
ex_zh: The unfathomable can be approached with wonder, not fear.

quote_en: The marathon runner showed unflagging energy, crossing the finish line with a smile.
quote_zh: My parents gave unflagging support through every school play and soccer game.
ex_en: Unflagging commitment to what is right is a superpower.
ex_zh: Be unflagging in kindness, even when it’s not returned; it changes you.

quote_en: The unflappable pilot landed the plane safely even after an engine failure.
quote_zh: Her unflappable demeanor kept the team calm when the project deadline was moved up.
ex_en: To be unflappable is to have a center that doesn’t shake.
ex_zh: Practice being unflappable: breathe, think, act.

quote_en: The penguin’s ungainly walk on land becomes graceful once it hits the water.
quote_zh: The ungainly teenager tripped over his own feet, but soon grew into his body.
ex_en: Ungainly can become graceful with practice and patience.
ex_zh: We are all ungainly in some things; that’s how we learn.

quote_en: The weather in spring is unpredictable—sunshine, then rain, then hail in one afternoon.
quote_zh: The championship game had an unpredictable outcome; the underdog won in overtime.
ex_en: Life’s unpredictability is not a curse; it’s the space where surprises live.
ex_zh: Embrace the unpredictable; it’s where adventure begins.

quote_en: The unruly crowd pushed toward the stage, so security formed a barrier.
quote_zh: My hair is so unruly that even gel can’t tame it on humid days.
ex_en: An unruly spirit can be channeled into creative energy.
ex_zh: Rules are for safety; kindness can calm the most unruly heart.

quote_en: The detective was suspicious of the unsavoury character lurking by the warehouse.
quote_zh: The restaurant had an unsavoury reputation for serving expired food.
ex_en: Avoid unsavoury shortcuts; they leave a taste that lingers.
ex_zh: What is unsavoury to one may be misunderstood; seek to understand before judging.

quote_en: We had unseasonable warmth in February, so we played outside in T-shirts.
quote_zh: The unseasonable snow in April surprised everyone and delayed the baseball season.
ex_en: Unseasonable events remind us that nature follows its own rules; so does life.
ex_zh: Adapt to the unseasonable with flexibility and a sense of humor.

quote_en: Her unwavering commitment to the cause inspired others to join.
quote_zh: The dog’s unwavering loyalty meant he waited by the door every day for his owner’s return.
ex_en: Unwavering doesn’t mean unchanging; it means staying true to what matters.
ex_zh: Be unwavering in kindness, even when the world seems otherwise.

quote_en: The teacher asked what we did over break; I gave a vague answer: “It was fun.”
quote_zh: I have a vague memory of visiting the ocean when I was very young.
ex_en: A vague promise is a seed that may or may not grow; a clear promise is a tree you can see.
ex_zh: When you need clarity, ask; don’t settle for vague.

quote_en: For the talent show, I wrote a verse about friendship that rhymed and made people smile.
quote_zh: My grandmother could recite biblical verses from memory, finding comfort in the words.
ex_en: A verse can hold a world of feeling in a few lines.
ex_zh: Let your life be a verse of kindness that others remember.

quote_en: In art class, we drew vertical lines to make a fence that looked like it went up.
quote_zh: The rock climber made a vertical climb up the cliff, hand over hand.
ex_en: Stand vertical to your values; don’t lean with every passing wind.
ex_zh: A vertical life is one of integrity—straight, true, unshaken.

def_zh: virtue of patience; virtue of honesty
quote_en: My grandmother taught me the virtue of patience by showing how to wait for bread to rise.
quote_zh: The virtue of honesty is that you never have to remember what you said.
ex_en: Virtue is not about being perfect; it’s about trying to be better than yesterday.
ex_zh: The greatest virtue is kindness; it makes all others shine.

quote_en: Steve Jobs was a visionary leader who imagined a computer in every pocket.
quote_zh: The visionary artist painted scenes of cities that didn’t exist yet.
ex_en: A visionary sees what is not yet visible; they plant seeds for others to harvest.
ex_zh: Be visionary in your dreams; be practical in your steps toward them.

quote_en: I wear a baseball visor to keep the sun out of my eyes during games.
quote_zh: The car’s sun visor flipped down to block the glare of the sunset.
ex_en: A visor protects your eyes; a good attitude protects your spirit.
ex_zh: Sometimes you need to raise your visor to see clearly; sometimes you need it for focus.

quote_en: From the top of the hike, we had a mountain vista of valleys and distant peaks.
quote_zh: The hotel room offered a panoramic vista of the ocean, stretching to the horizon.
ex_en: A vista rewards the climb; don’t stop before the view.
ex_zh: Life’s vistas are best shared; call someone to see what you see.

quote_en: Oranges are rich in vitamin C, which helps your immune system fight colds.
quote_zh: A vitamin deficiency can make you tired, so eat a rainbow of fruits and vegetables.
ex_en: Vitamins feed the body; books feed the mind; kindness feeds the soul.
ex_zh: The best vitamin for a gloomy day is a dose of laughter.

quote_en: The princess wore a voluminous skirt that spread wide when she twirled.
quote_zh: The library has voluminous records of the town’s history dating back centuries.
ex_en: Voluminous knowledge is impressive; applied wisdom is powerful.
ex_zh: Let your heart be voluminous enough to hold all who need its warmth.

quote_en: The windward side of the mountain gets more rain, so it’s lush and green.
quote_zh: The windward islands are named because they face the prevailing trade winds.
ex_en: Stand windward to the storms of life; they will strengthen you.
ex_zh: Sometimes you must sail windward to reach your destination; it’s harder but worth it.

def_zh: ball of yarn; spin a yarn
quote_en: Grandma’s cat loves to play with a ball of yarn, batting it across the floor.
quote_zh: My grandfather would spin a yarn about his childhood, making us laugh with his tall tales.
ex_en: Yarn can weave cloth; stories can weave community.
ex_zh: Spin your own yarn—your life is the thread, and your choices are the pattern.

quote_en: In zoology class, we dissected owl pellets to see what they ate.
quote_zh: I want to study zoology in college so I can work with endangered species.
ex_en: Zoology teaches us that every creature has a place, and we share one Earth.
ex_zh: The wonder of zoology is that the more you learn, the more you realize how much there is to discover.
